process_name: Rare semileptonic decay D+ -> pi+ mu+ mu-
implementation_difficulty_reason: Needs a new Delta C = 1 semileptonic operator/matching layer plus a D -> pi mu+ mu- branching-fraction calculation with form factors, phase-space/resonance treatment, and recast conventions.

commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests docs scripts -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests docs scripts -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests docs scripts -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "D\\+.*pi\\+.*mu|D\\^\\+.*pi\\^\\+.*mu|D\\+.*pimumu|DTopimumu|pi\\+.*mu\\+.*mu|c.?->.?u|Delta.?C.?=.?1|rare.?charm|semileptonic.*charm|C_9|C9|C_10|C10|Wilson.*charm" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: Focused implementation-dir searches found no D+ -> pi+ mu+ mu- or Delta C = 1 rare semileptonic charm observable implementation.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:252 and quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941 are D0 mixing hits, not D+ -> pi+ mu+ mu-.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 and quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:668 are D0 mixing policy/evaluator hits, not rare semileptonic charm.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 is the separate mu -> e gamma LFV dipole check.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:}
\(\mathcal{B}(D^+\to\pi^+\mu^+\mu^-)\).
This entry covers the rare charged-charm semileptonic decay
\(D^+\to\pi^+\mu^+\mu^-\), a \(\Delta C=1\), \(c\to u\mu^+\mu^-\)
flavor-changing neutral-current probe.  Charge-conjugate modes are implied by
the experimental sources.
sections.pdg_prose:
The current PDG Live/API entry S031.42, accessed on 2026-05-16, lists
\[
  \mathcal{B}(D^+\to\pi^+\mu^+\mu^-) < 6.7\times 10^{-8}
  \qquad (90\%~{\rm C.L.})
\]
from the LHCb 2021 search in \(1.6\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) of 2016 \(pp\) data.
The LHCb source table gives the companion 95\% C.L. limit
\(7.4\times10^{-8}\).  The predecessor LHCb 2013 nonresonant search, using
\(1.0\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) at \(\sqrt{s}=7\,{\rm TeV}\), reported
\(7.3(8.3)\times10^{-8}\) at 90\% (95\%) C.L.  These numbers are traced to
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/C007/pdg2026\_dplus\_piplus\_mumu\_pdgLive.txt},
\texttt{lhcb2021\_arxiv2011\_00217.txt}, and
\texttt{lhcb2013\_arxiv1304\_6365.txt}.
sections.relevance:
Anarchic warped models can induce up-sector flavor-changing neutral currents
through nonuniversal KK gauge couplings, \(Z\)-like flavor violation, Higgs or
radion scalar effects, and lepton-current extensions.  This process is the
charged semileptonic \(\Delta C=1\) analogue of the implemented neutral
\(D^0\)-mixing lane: it probes \(c\to u\) vector, axial-vector, and possible
scalar semileptonic operators rather than four-quark \(\Delta F=2\) operators.
It is therefore a roadmap constraint for a broader flavor backend, not a
constraint currently applied by the quark scan.
sections.post_2008:
The arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline predates the modern LHCb rare-charm
program.  Since then, LHCb first set nonresonant \(D^+\to\pi^+\mu^+\mu^-\)
limits at the \(10^{-8}\) level in 2013 and then folded the channel into a
2021 search for 25 rare or forbidden \(D^+\) and \(D_s^+\) decays.  The 2021
analysis found no evidence in any of the investigated modes and tightened the
PDG-listed C007 limit to \(6.7\times10^{-8}\) at 90\% C.L.  On the theory side,
post-2008 rare-charm EFT work, especially de Boer and Hiller 2015, clarified
that \(D_{(s)}\to P\ell^+\ell^-\) branching-ratio recasts must separate
short-distance Wilson coefficients from resonance-dominated long-distance
physics; the LHCb 2021 introduction quotes short-distance SM branching
fractions of order \(10^{-12}\), with dilepton resonances dominating the
physical spectrum.
sections.validity:
The experimental upper limit is robust as a counting result, but its use as an
RS short-distance bound is model dependent.  Long-distance hadronic resonances,
weak-annihilation effects, and the exact phase-space definition of the
``nonresonant'' signal matter.  A conservative catalog use is an upper bound on
additional short-distance \(c\to u\mu^+\mu^-\) contributions in the same
experimental region, with an explicit recast needed before treating it as a
hard scan veto.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The required catalog greps and a focused search for
\texttt{D+.*pi+.*mu}, \texttt{c.?->.?u}, \texttt{rare.?charm},
\texttt{semileptonic.*charm}, \texttt{C9}, and \texttt{C10} found no
\(D^+\to\pi^+\mu^+\mu^-\) implementation in \texttt{quarkConstraints/},
\texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/},
\texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, or \texttt{tests/}.  Nearby hits are unrelated:
\(D^0\) mixing appears at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:252} and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941}, the modern policy lists only
\(D^0\) mixing at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23} and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:668}, and
\(\mu\to e\gamma\) is separate at
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  A production implementation needs a new \(\Delta C=1\)
semileptonic operator basis and matching for \(C_{9,10}^{(\prime)}\) and
possible scalar/tensor terms, plus a \(D\to\pi\mu^+\mu^-\) mode calculation
with form factors, phase-space cuts, and a documented treatment of resonant
long-distance contributions.
sections.key_references:
Process-local keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{PDG2026:DPlusPiMuMu}, \texttt{LHCb2021:DPlusPiMuMu},
\texttt{LHCb2013:DPlusPiMuMu},
\texttt{DeBoerHiller2015:RareCharmLeptons}, and
\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}.

L11: 
L12: Abstract-page metadata extract:

L26: transitions. These processes are rare in the Standard Model because only
L27: weak-annihilation processes can occur at tree level. At the loop level,
L28: FCNC transitions are GIM suppressed. The source states that short-distance
L29: SM branching fractions are of order 10^-12, while significant long-distance
L30: contributions from dilepton resonances are expected to dominate across the
L31: dilepton invariant-mass-squared distribution. The analysis uses 1.6 fb^-1
L32: of 2016 LHCb data. Four resonant D_(s)+ -> pi+ phi(->ll) decays are used

priority_rationale_short: Best charm semileptonic FCNC probe, but recast is model-dependent.
priority_rationale_full:
D+->pi+mu+mu- probes c->u semileptonic FCNC operators that are complementary
to Delta C=2 D mixing. The LHCb/PDG bound is useful, but long-distance
resonances and the nonresonant phase-space definition prevent a clean
universal veto. It is a MEDIUM roadmap constraint for broader RS flavor fits.
